titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Keith Fleming Papers Relating to Edmund White
Fleming (Keith) Papers Relating to Edmund White
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 10, 2019 at 03:01
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.unittitle: Keith Fleming Papers Relating to Edmund White

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 2-3 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 4 (computer disk): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Keith Fleming Papers Relating to Edmund White is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Keith Fleming Papers Relating to Edmund White. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing in 2014, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, identified by a unique call number and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

The finding aid for this collection is compiled from individual preliminary lists for each acquisition that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2007-2008. As part of the migration, modifications were made to the formatting of individual lists; however, the content of the lists was neither modified nor verified.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Keith Fleming
bioghist.p: Keith Fleming, American writer, is the author of The Boy with the Thorn in His Side (New York: W. Morrow, 2000) and Original Youth: The Real Story of Edmund White's Boyhood (San Francisco, Calif.: Green Candy Press, 2003). Keith Fleming is Edmund White's nephew.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Drafts, 1993-1999. II. Research Materials, 1995, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Keith Fleming Papers Relating to Edmund White contain manuscripts, research files, biographical material, photographs, sound recordings of interviews, and electronic media created and collected by Fleming while working on the biography Original Youth: The Real Story of Edmund White's Boyhood .

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Keith Fleming on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2001.

did.unittitle: 2. Loose material on people interviewed including bio info by others
3. Loose material I've gathered on Ed
7. To be read of Ed's (or on him)
25. Copies of my letters to people
26-27. [empty] and [My Life in the Basement] - empty
